health_zone_table.basis: SitRep48 Table 2 (01 July 2026) visually reviewed against the INSP PDF (post 25076; SitRep_MVE_RDC_20260701_OK.pdf sha256 8e22f356abc231471fa8f23ceb5b0789d9b8b3ecb87d4219afb8d4a02c7f41e5, 14 pages).

reconciliation.note: Rows promoted here reconcile to official province/national totals: 1443 named confirmed cases plus 17 Ituri cases awaiting ventilation = 1460; all 452 confirmed deaths are named-zone attributed. The residual row is retained as unventilated context and excluded from mappable named-zone attribution.

lab_indicators_24h.note: National aggregate from SitRep48 section 4.4: Ituri 132 collected/analyzed / 50 positive (37.9%); Nord-Kivu 96 collected/analyzed / 4 positive (4.2%); Sud-Kivu 2 collected, 0 analyzed (2 under analysis). Positivity uses 54 positives over 228 analyzed samples.

care_continuity: End-of-day isolation stood at 641 patients at 97.8% global occupancy (up from 609 at 92.7% at SitRep47). SitRep48 published no patient-movement (CTE/CT/CI) table, so the confirmed/suspected split is reconciled to the fresh census by carrying confirmed-in-isolation at 216 (last published, SitRep46) and setting suspected-in-isolation to the 425 residual; it is flagged as a carried/reconciled estimate rather than a freshly reported split.

From the holistic-care section, 21 patients were declared recovered (19 Ituri, 2 Nord-Kivu) and 14 deaths were recorded among previously confirmed CTE patients in Ituri during the cycle. Continuity of care reports 102 confirmed people-deprived-of-liberty (PPL) cumulative, of whom 25 died (24.5%) and 36 recovered.
highlights: 54 new confirmed cases including 9 deaths were reported for 2026-07-01, in Ituri (50 including 9 deaths) and Nord-Kivu (4).
In Nord-Kivu the 4 new cases were in Katwa (2) and Beni (2); Ituri contributed the remaining 50 new cases.
No new affected health zone was reported on 2026-07-01; affected zones remain 36/104 and Ituri stays at 24/36 zones touched. Sud-Kivu has recorded no confirmed case since 2026-05-26.

14 deaths were recorded among previously confirmed CTE patients in Ituri (5 notified in CTEs: 3 CTE Bunia, 1 CTE Mongbwalu, 1 CTE Rwampara; 9 community deaths), bringing confirmed deaths to 452. 21 patients recovered (19 in Ituri: 7 CTE Elikya, 6 CTE Mongbwalu, 4 CTE ISTM, 2 CTE CME; 2 in Nord-Kivu at CTE Katwa), bringing DRC cumulative recoveries to 213.

The 5 imported cases at Wamba (Haut-Uele province) are counted within the Nia-Nia health zone (Ituri) and were returned to Nia-Nia; the affected-province count stays at 3 and there is no separate Haut-Uele denominator.
laboratory: SitRep48 reports Ituri collected and analyzed 132 samples with 50 positive (37.9%).
Nord-Kivu collected and analyzed 96 samples with 4 positive (4.2%). Sud-Kivu collected 2 samples, still under analysis.
publication_context: INSP SitRep 48 reports through 2026-07-01 and was published on the INSP WordPress site (post 25076; embedded PDF media 25077; WP publish 2026-07-03T06:22:59Z). The PDF title is SitRep N48/MVB_01/07/2026.
SitRep48 supersedes SitRep47 directly (a single report-day advance); the cumulative rose by 54 confirmed and 14 deaths over 2026-07-01.
The outbreak is reported across 3 provinces and 36 of 104 health zones; no newly affected zone was reported this SitRep.
Ituri remains the epicenter with 1333 of 1460 DRC confirmed cases (91.3%) and 380 deaths (CFR 28.5%).
Nord-Kivu holds the highest provincial confirmed-case fatality ratio at 57.3%, led by Katwa (67.3%), Oicha (66.7%) and Beni (57.7%); 100% lethality observed in low-count Vuhovi.
redactions: Page contact details and operational names, if present outside table facts, are intentionally not promoted into public evidence text.
surveillance: 958 alerts were reported and 842 investigated, producing an 87.9% investigation rate; Ituri 48.7% (110/226), Nord-Kivu 100.0% (723/723), Sud-Kivu 100.0% (9/9).
150 suspected cases of the day were identified, including 41 deceased validated alerts.
10,821 contacts were under follow-up and 8,954 were seen, producing 82.7% follow-up coverage (completeness 93.3%; Logo and Nia-Nia in Ituri had no data at consolidation).

220 new contacts were listed (89 across 4 Ituri health zones and 131 across 3 Nord-Kivu health zones); 204 contacts completed 21-day follow-up (89 in Ituri, 115 in Nord-Kivu); 16 contacts became suspects (9 Ituri, 7 Nord-Kivu). Follow-up of the France case contacts continues in Kinshasa at 67.3% (72 of 107 seen), at day 10.

alerts_total.data_gap_note: Report (J-1) 73; new alerts 885; total alerts of the day 958. 220 new contacts listed (89 in 4 Ituri HZs, 131 in 3 Nord-Kivu HZs); 204 completed 21-day follow-up (89 Ituri, 115 Nord-Kivu).

challenges.finding: Community reluctance toward post-mortem sampling (EDS/swabs) and resistance delay confirmation, underestimate cases, and disrupt key activities.
challenges.response: Intensify RCCE, involve religious and community leaders, and reinforce team security.

challenges.finding: Insufficient CTE capacity with Nord-Kivu saturation cited at 138.8% in the challenge table; the banner reports national isolation occupancy 97.8% (641 patients), raising nosocomial-transmission risk.
challenges.response: Accelerate CTE construction/expansion and deploy additional temporary isolation structures.

challenges.finding: Contact follow-up performance reached 82.7% (challenge table cites 81.3%) versus the 95% target, leaving transmission chains uninterrupted.
challenges.response: Train, deploy, and motivate community relays (RECO) with reinforced supervision.

challenges.finding: Weak alert reporting in some affected health zones risks undetected, silent transmission.
challenges.response: Reinforce community-based surveillance and rapid alert investigation.

challenges.finding: Seventeen Ituri confirmed cases remain not yet ventilated by health zone, leaving gaps in transmission-chain reconstruction.
challenges.response: Conduct data reconciliation, prune duplicates, and reclassify/localize with active field verification.

challenges.finding: Insufficient supply of essential medicines (MEG) and medical inputs weakens holistic case management.
challenges.response: Advocacy and urgent procurement of essential medicines.

challenges.finding: Insufficient IPC inputs (PPE, chlorine) and limited training raise nosocomial-infection risk; the challenge table reports 96 health-worker infections and 19 deaths (CFR 19.7%).
challenges.response: Urgent procurement and reinforced IPC training.

challenges.finding: A backlog of un-analyzed samples in Nord-Kivu and late diagnosis persist amid a 54.2% provincial CFR (challenge table).
challenges.response: Deploy a laboratory catch-up plan and reinforce diagnostic capacity.

challenges.finding: Insufficient ambulances and isolation structures (a gap of about 20 centers) delay transfer and isolation and raise transmission risk.
challenges.response: Urgent deployment of logistics and additional infrastructure.

challenges.finding: Insufficient coordination and weak use of COUSP frameworks cause operational inefficiency and delayed decisions.
challenges.response: Reinforce coordination mechanisms at all levels and operationalize provincial COUSP.

challenges.finding: A financial-resource gap of about USD 20 million limits implementation across all response pillars.
challenges.response: Urgent resource mobilization and partner advocacy.

challenges.finding: Insecurity and limited access (CODECO, ADF) restrict operations and access to high-risk zones.
challenges.response: Strengthen security coordination and humanitarian access.

challenges.finding: Compromised continuity of essential (non-Ebola) health services raises indirect mortality.
challenges.response: Effectively implement free care and reinforce continuity of essential health services (CEHS).

challenges.finding: High population mobility raises the risk of rapid geographic expansion; this cycle 5 imported cases at Wamba (Haut-Uele) were counted within Nia-Nia, and a security incident was reported at PK 51 in Nia-Nia (Ituri).
challenges.response: Reinforce PoE/PoC surveillance and cross-border/inter-provincial coordination.

contacts_total.data_gap_note: Follow-up rate computed only for reporting ZS (completeness 93.3%); Logo and Nia-Nia (Ituri) had no data at consolidation. Sud-Kivu contacts remain exited. 220 new contacts listed (89 in 4 Ituri HZs, 131 in 3 Nord-Kivu HZs); 204 completed 21-day follow-up (89 Ituri, 115 Nord-Kivu); 16 became suspects (9 Ituri, 7 Nord-Kivu). France case contacts followed in Kinshasa at 67.3% (72/107 seen), day 10.

patient_movement_total.data_gap_note: SitRep48 published no patient-movement (CTE/CT/CI) table; the banner reports only the aggregate isolation census 641 at 97.8% occupancy (up from 609 at 92.7% at SitRep47). Confirmed-in-isolation is carried at its last published value (216, SitRep46) and the +32 census growth is attributed to suspected-in-isolation (425 residual), so the split reconciles to the fresh 641 census; the split is a carried/reconciled estimate, not a freshly reported table. Exit flows sourced from the holistic-care section: 21 recoveries (19 Ituri, 2 Nord-Kivu) and 14 deaths among previously confirmed CTE patients in Ituri; other 24h movement flows were not published and are shown as 0.

priorities.priority: Resolve the 17 unventilated Ituri confirmed cases without distributing them to named health zones; keep the Wamba/Haut-Uele imported cases counted within Nia-Nia

Relieve isolation load at 97.8% national occupancy and the Nord-Kivu 57.3% CFR; close the reported gap of about 20 isolation centers and accelerate CTE construction (new CTE site identified in Bunia; Nizi and Nyankunde sites evaluated)
Improve contact follow-up (82.7% vs 95% target), reinforce intervention teams and transport in affected ZS, and clear the Nord-Kivu laboratory backlog

preserved_conflicts: SitRep48 headline is DRC-only (1460 confirmed / 452 confirmed deaths across Ituri, Nord-Kivu, Sud-Kivu); the country-scope public snapshot adds the latest reviewed Uganda component separately.

The 1,460 confirmed total is marked re-actualized and subject to change after DHIS2 cleaning/reclassification/harmonization; SitRep48 supersedes SitRep47 directly (a single report-day advance), so treat the +54 confirmed and +14 confirmed deaths vs SitRep47 as an official one-day delta for 2026-07-01.
SitRep48 carries an explicit Ituri residual of 17 confirmed cases in zones not yet identified; these counts are public but not mappable to named health zones.
No new affected health zone was reported this cycle; the affected-zone count remains 36 of 104 (Ituri 24/36). No new map geometry was required.

Five imported cases in Wamba ZS (Haut-Uele province) are administratively counted within the Nia-Nia health zone (Ituri) and were returned to Nia-Nia; the affected-province count remains 3 and there is no separate Haut-Uele denominator.

The isolation census (641 at 97.8% occupancy, up from 609 at 92.7%) is reported only in the SitRep48 banner; SitRep48 published no patient-movement (CTE/CT/CI) table, so the confirmed/suspected split (216 confirmed / 425 suspected) is reconciled to the fresh census by carrying confirmed-in-isolation at its last published value (216, SitRep46) and attributing the +32 census growth to suspected-in-isolation; it is flagged as carried/reconciled, not freshly reported. From the holistic-care section, 21 recoveries and 14 CTE deaths occurred this cycle.
Daily suspected cases (150) and the 41 deceased validated alerts are current-cycle validated alert outputs, not cumulative suspected stock or cumulative confirmed deaths.

Uganda anchor remains 20 cumulative confirmed cases (2 deaths, 11 recoveries) per the Uganda MoH EVD daily dashboard (last reviewed 2026-06-21); WHO AFRO Uganda External SitRep 07 (data as of 2026-06-28) independently reports no new confirmed Uganda cases over the past week. Carried forward this cycle as INSP reports DRC only, so country-scope is 1480 confirmed / 454 deaths / 224 recovered.

Sud-Kivu has recorded no new confirmed case since 2026-05-26, suggesting no recent transmission; it remains at 3 confirmed / 1 death across Miti-Murhesa, and its contacts remain exited from follow-up.
Several low-denominator zones report 100% case-fatality this cycle (Drodro 3/3, Kambala 2/2, Vuhovi 1/1, Lolwa 1/1); these are unstable small-number ratios.

External response milestones reported outside the INSP SitRep are tracked as separate context in the LOVS narrative/synthesis, not in the INSP denominators: the WHO Emergency Use Listing of the first BDBV molecular RT-PCR diagnostic (2026-07-02) supports the SitRep-noted decentralization of diagnostic capacity; and the response financing picture (INSP-cited ~USD 20 million pillar-implementation gap; separately, an Africa CDC response requirement of ~USD 1.4 billion with pledges only ~13% released, a White House/OMB supplemental request of ~USD 1.4 billion still before Congress, and OCHA/UNDP macro-humanitarian and socioeconomic context) is recorded as distinct facets and not collapsed into a single figure.

review_notes: SitRep #048 (report date 2026-07-01; INSP WordPress post 25076 published 2026-07-03T06:22:59Z; embedded PDF media 25077). PDF sha256 8e22f356abc231471fa8f23ceb5b0789d9b8b3ecb87d4219afb8d4a02c7f41e5, 14 pages. Title N48/MVB_01/07/2026.

SitRep48 supersedes SitRep47 directly (single report-day). DRC headline 1460 confirmed / 452 confirmed deaths (CFR 30.9%); +54 confirmed and +14 confirmed deaths vs SitRep47. On 01 July, 54 new confirmed (Ituri 50, Nord-Kivu 4) carried 9 deaths; a further set of deaths occurred among previously confirmed CTE patients (14 in Ituri; Nord-Kivu deaths unchanged at 71).
The 1,460 total is flagged re-actualized and subject to change after DHIS2 cleaning/reclassification; deltas are official revised deltas.
No new affected health zone this cycle. Affected-zone count remains 36 of 104; Ituri stays 24/36 touched. No new map geometry/centroid required.
Health-zone rows reconcile to 1460 confirmed / 452 deaths: 1443 named confirmed cases plus 17 Ituri cases awaiting ventilation, with all 452 confirmed deaths named-zone attributed.
Five imported cases in Wamba ZS (Haut-Uele) are counted within Nia-Nia (Ituri) and were returned to Nia-Nia. The affected-province count stays at 3 and there is no separate Haut-Uele denominator.

Recovered advanced to 213 DRC cumulative (+5). Uganda anchor carried forward at 20 cumulative cases / 2 deaths / 11 recoveries (last reviewed 2026-06-21; WHO Uganda External SitRep 07 corroborates no new Uganda transmission as of 2026-06-28), so country-scope is 1480 confirmed / 454 deaths / 224 recovered.

Laboratory throughput promoted from section 4.4: Ituri 132 collected/analyzed, 50 positive (37.9%); Nord-Kivu 96 collected/analyzed, 4 positive (4.2%); Sud-Kivu 2 collected/under analysis (228 analyzed / 54 positive overall, 23.7%).

Operational data captured: alerts 958 reported / 842 investigated (87.9%), 150 suspected cases of the day (41 deceased validated alerts), 10,821 contacts under follow-up (82.7%), 220 new contacts, 204 completed 21-day, PoC activation 68/69. SitRep48 published no patient-movement table; the isolation census (641, 97.8%) is fresh but the confirmed/suspected split (216/393) is carried from SitRep46 and flagged.

External context recorded separately (not in INSP denominators): WHO Emergency Use Listing of the first BDBV molecular diagnostic (2026-07-02) aligns with the SitRep-noted diagnostic decentralization; financing facets (INSP ~USD 20M pillar gap; Africa CDC ~USD 1.4B requirement / ~13% released; White House/OMB ~USD 1.4B request pending; OCHA/UNDP macro context) are kept as distinct facets.
